changed_files.note: Full rewrite of the 6.1.1 placeholder. Defines the disclosed /graph wire contract in its header comment (lines ~1-33): GET /graph?path=<encoded path> -> { path, neighbors: [{ path, edgeType, weight, hop }] }.
New file, 5 test cases: neighbor-count render (3 neighbors), ?path= deep-link auto-fetch (1 neighbor), empty-neighbors state, error state (404), empty-path submit guard.
One-line data-testid change in the routes table: graph-status -> graph-form (line ~24), following the identical precedent already documented in that file's own comment for the /query row from 6.1.2.

acceptance_criteria: Selecting a file shows its graph neighbors (from /graph) as a visual adjacency/traversal view.
Test spec: component test mocking a /graph response, asserting the expected number of neighbor nodes render.

test_spec_pointer.primary_test: "renders the expected number of neighbor nodes after selecting a file" -- mocks 3 neighbors, asserts screen.findAllByTestId("graph-neighbor") has length 3

disclosed_gaps_for_verifier_attention: No Go /graph HTTP handler exists in api/routes/ (only /query does, via api/routes/query.go). This subtask's fetch contract (GET /graph?path=<path> -> {path, neighbors:[{path, edgeType, weight, hop}]}) is a UI-side design decision, not derived from an existing backend contract -- documented at the top of ui/src/routes/GraphView.tsx and in architecture-discovery.md. Verifier should confirm this is an acceptable, clearly-disclosed simplification given the subtask is UI-only, same category of gap query.go itself disclosed before 6.1.2.

Path-based (not fileID-based) neighbor identification: proto/hivemind.proto's GraphNeighborsResponse.Neighbor uses target_file_id, not a path. This view uses path strings throughout to stay consistent with QueryView's citation-link convention (/files?path=...). Verifier should judge whether this is a reasonable adaptation or should have been flagged as a bigger open question.

App.test.tsx was touched even though not listed in the issue's 'Impacted modules' for 6.1.3 -- justified by the identical precedent 6.1.2 set (commit 71cb1bb) for the exact same situation (query-status -> query-form).
local_verification_note: Implementation agent ran `npm run build` and `npm test` inside ui/ locally (self-consistency step, NOT verification): both passed -- build produced dist/ with no errors; vitest run reported 3 test files / 14 tests, all passing. See self-consistency.json for details. This is not an adversarial verification pass.
